title: Aureline M1 schema-registry seed row
description: Boundary schema for one row in the focused M1 schema-registry seed at schemas/registry/schema_registry.yaml. Each row names one telemetry, diagnostics, support-export, or usage-export payload family that later M1 privacy, support, and release work must consume rather than re-inventing a private JSON shape. The row inherits its broader consent, endpoint, retention, build-flavor, downgrade, and exclusion posture from the canonical schema_registry_entry_row in schemas/governance/schema_registry_entry.schema.json (via consent_ledger_entry_id_ref) and from the record-class registry; it does NOT redeclare those axes. What this row freezes is the M1-bearing subset: owner, schema path, pinned schema version, lifecycle state, default posture, consent and redaction class summaries, retention note, compatibility horizon, deprecated-field tracking, and exactly one canonical example payload that the validation lane re-parses end-to-end. Adding an enum value to family_class, lifecycle_state_class, consent_class, default_posture_class, endpoint_class, redaction_class, or registry_consumer_class is additive-minor and bumps schema_registry_row_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

schema_registry_row_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this M1 schema-registry-row vocabulary. Bumped only on breaking row changes.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence safe for docs, packets, and the validation lane capture.

path_ref.description: Repo-relative path or document reference. Consumers treat it as opaque text.

owner_dri.description: DRI owning team or maintainer handle in '@handle' form.

family_class.description: Closed family vocabulary for the M1 seed. Telemetry, diagnostics, support-export, and usage-export payload families are kept as distinct rows even when they share transport, redaction, or export plumbing.

lifecycle_state_class.description: Lifecycle state of the row's pinned schema family. M1 rows are 'seeded' or 'stabilising'; later milestones move rows to 'stable' once the consumer surfaces are live.

consent_class.description: Consent posture summary; the canonical row in consent_ledger_seed.yaml carries the full closed vocabulary. The M1 seed asserts the value here matches the canonical row.

default_posture_class.description: Default posture summary the M1 seed enforces. Distinguishes telemetry's off-by-default-until-consent posture from diagnostics' local-capture-only posture from export-only flows.

endpoint_class.description: Where the payload may travel. The closed set is the M1 subset of the canonical endpoint vocabulary.

redaction_class.description: Default redaction posture the payload family inherits. The canonical bytes-level redaction contract remains ADR 0007; this row names the family's default class.

registry_consumer_class.description: What kind of consumer the row's named runtime consumer is. The M1 seed requires at least one consumer per row.

field_path.description: Dotted JSON path of the deprecated field within the family's schema (for example 'envelope.session_token').

replacement_field_path.description: Optional replacement field path that takes over the meaning.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the M1 schema-registry row shape.

entry_id.description: Stable dot-separated id for this M1 registry row. Mint a new id rather than reusing one.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of what the family is, what it does not cover, and how it is distinguished from siblings it shares plumbing with.

schema_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the family's pinned schema file.

schema_version_pin.description: Pinned integer schema_version of the family schema. The validation lane asserts the example payload encodes this exact version.

schema_version_uri.description: Stable $id URI the schema document publishes; consumers cite verbatim.

retention_note.description: Short retention note: how long the payload lives and what closes the window. Authoritative posture is inherited from the record-class registry; this is the M1-bearing summary.

deprecated_fields.description: Tracked deprecated fields for the family. Empty when nothing is deprecated yet.

consent_ledger_entry_id_ref.description: entry_id of the row in artifacts/governance/consent_ledger_seed.yaml the M1 row inherits its full consent/endpoint/retention vocabulary from. The validation lane asserts the consent_class agrees with the referenced canonical row.

example_payload_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the canonical example payload (JSON) under fixtures/schemas/m1_registry_examples/. The example MUST carry the family's record_kind discriminator and the pinned schema_version.
